title: Shame on you Angelina Jolie
body:
Shame on you Angelina Jolie & Christina Applegate! You use your celebrity status to create fear mongering and paranoia. You are causing many young and healthy women to consider taking drastic steps to prevent a cancer they may never get. 

The odds of a healthy woman to get breast cancer is 12% (120 in 1000). The BRCA1 mutation has the odds to exist in 1 of 500 cases (0.2% chance). Think about these odds they are very low. I do not disagree, those that carry the BRCA1 mutation do have an increase of risk of getting the disease.  For those few that have the mutation the risk is 60% (600 in 1000) cases. However, you are influencing far more then the few women that have this risk. You are putting doubt and concerns into your minds that steers them towards a life altering procedure that may be completely unnecessary. 

Consider this: Canada has a female population of 9,365,000 aged 25-65 (as of Jan 2013), if 2% carry the mutation that equates to 18,730. Now consider that 60% could actually contract the disease, that would be 11,238.  11,238 in 9.36 million is 0.1%. 

Of those positive cases of breast cancer, many are detected with regular annual mammography screenings. Women should start screening at 40 or earlier if you have concerns.  Annual mammography screenings will allow you peace of mind and year over year history will add accuracy for detection.

I have sat and worked with countless radiologists and these medical professionals are experts at detection. You can be assured the annual screenings are thorough.  There is also plenty of literature which describes the screening process which I highly recommend to anyone else before deciding to proceed with a double mastectomy. 
That said, the BRCA1 mutation also drastically increases the risk of ovarian cancer as well. Are you planning on getting a Hysterectomy?  I didn't hear that in the news. Interesting that the line would be drawn here.  

Maybe you celebrities are just getting a big payoff for speaking up.  The procedure that is performed to test for the BRCA1 mutation is not something you can get done at your hospital. Particularly in Canada the BRCA1 test procedure is difficult to do. That is because a company called Myriad Genetics claims to own a patent on the procedure. The result is that you must pay the company to have the procedure done. Its price tag is $4000 US regardless of the results.  Imagine how much business Angelina Jolie is drumming up for Myriad Genetics. 

I get worked up about this because I have several years understanding the mammography screening process and worked with the dedicated personnel that have contributed to the improved cancer detection rates. I am seen the care, hard work and achievements made. It makes me furious when someone with some influence can come along and convince people to take away a part of themselves based on an uncertainty.

 My advice to anyone that heard the Angelina Jolie news releases today is to research into this and make you own decisions but don't do it because a celebrity told you to. 

I make this rant with confidence based on my several years experience working with a leading US organization in Diagnostic Imaging

Information I have presented are based on facts which can be supported by the following web sites:

US National Cancer Institute
http://m.cancer.gov/topics/factsheets/BRCA

Wikipedia: Myriad Genetics
http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Myriad_Genetics

Index Mundi demographics
http://www.indexmundi.com/canada/demographics_profile.html

Statistics Canada
http://www.statcan.gc.ca/start-debut-eng.html
